demographics.living_situation: lives at home; adult child present and made the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (call made by the adult child) She hasn't made sense for two days, and today I can't wake her up at all. Her skin is completely dried out. She's diabetic and takes tablets but she's been so confused she keeps missing them.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's been building for a couple of weeks — she's been weeing constantly, drinking non-stop and exhausted — but today I can't rouse her and I'm frightened.

symptom_narrative.onset: gradual over about two weeks — huge amounts of urine, endless thirst and terrible tiredness — then over the last two days she became confused and drowsy
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly two weeks, sharply worse in the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: she was passing enormous amounts of urine and drinking constantly, then got muddled and sleepy, and today she won't wake at all and her skin looks parched
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst she's ever been — completely unrousable
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: she's just not responsive; her whole body is limp
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it has just kept getting worse on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: earlier today her right arm seemed weak for a while; no vomiting, no tummy pain, no fever that we noticed
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bedbound, cannot be woken, completely dependent

past_medical_history.how_managed: oral diabetes tablets, but she's missed a lot lately because she's been confused

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: elderly, lives at home; adult child is present and reports the blister packs show doses missed over the past week

objective_data_offered.value: her home meter just says HIGH — it won't give a number, it says it's over 33.3
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: home glucometer reading above the meter's measurable ceiling; patient-obtained and unverified
home ketone meter, single reading, patient-obtained and unverified

objective_data_offered.value: I cannot wake her at all — she doesn't respond when I call her name or shake her
her eyes look sunken and her lips and mouth are completely dry and cracked; her skin looks parched
earlier today her right arm went weak for a little while, then seemed to settle
